GK-LAV-001.name: Lavender
GK-LAV-001.subtitle: Scented, sun-loving, pollinator friendly.
GK-LAV-001.notes: Do not overwater. Lavender dislikes sitting in wet compost. Add grit or perlite if the potting mix is too rich or heavy.

seasonalActions.Spring: Check drainage and remove dead growth.
seasonalActions.Summer: Trim lightly after flowering and avoid heavy feeding.
seasonalActions.Autumn: Reduce watering and keep the crown dry.
seasonalActions.Winter: Protect from sitting in wet, cold compost.

problems: Leggy growth usually means not enough sun or too much feed.

GK-TOM-001.name: Tomato
GK-TOM-001.subtitle: Warm-season edible crop for sunny balconies, patios and greenhouses.

quickCare.Water: Keep evenly moist, avoid drying out
quickCare.Pruning: Remove side shoots on cordon types

GK-TOM-001.notes: Tomatoes are hungry plants. Keep watering consistent to reduce splitting and blossom end rot. Stake or support as the plant grows.

seasonalActions.Spring: Sow indoors or buy young plants after frost risk.
seasonalActions.Summer: Feed weekly, water regularly and tie in growth.
seasonalActions.Autumn: Ripen remaining fruit indoors if weather cools.
seasonalActions.Winter: Clean pots and dispose of old plant material.

problems: Blossom end rot is often linked to irregular watering.
Whitefly and aphids can build up under cover.

GK-FRN-001.name: Fern
GK-FRN-001.subtitle: Shade-loving foliage plant that prefers steady moisture.
GK-FRN-001.notes: Ferns generally prefer humidity and protection from harsh midday sun. Avoid letting the compost completely dry out.

seasonalActions.Spring: Remove old fronds and refresh top layer of compost.
seasonalActions.Summer: Watch moisture closely during warm weather.
seasonalActions.Winter: Keep away from cold draughts and avoid overwatering.

problems: Crispy tips often indicate low humidity or underwatering.
Yellow fronds may indicate overwatering or poor drainage.
